The '''Cadinth Oligarchy''' was the government that controlled the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Cadinth]] during the last [[Time/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], led by [[Prime Oligarch]] [[Rankwin Fopow]].<ref name="Article" /> In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="Date">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|55|jediwatch/1359_2.html|Cadinth Loyalty Uncertain After Jedi Death}} is set on the date 13:5:9, which corresponds to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] according to the reasoning [[Template:YearMathGRS|here]].</ref> during a [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|conflict]] between the Republic and a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|militant secessionist faction]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the [[Oligarchy]]'s loyalty lay with the former. In the wake of a pro-Separatist [[Saskapeg uprising|uprising]] in Cadinth's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Saskapeg]] that ended with the [[Death/Legends|death]] of the planet's [[Jedi Watchman/Legends|Jedi Watchman]], [[Bodis-Ker Vitan]], the Oligarchy retracted its previous support of the Republic. Fopow later withdrew all statements saying instead that the planet was "firmly undecided" between the two groups.<ref name="Article" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Cadinth Oligarchy was mentioned in [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 55|''HoloNet News Vol. 531 #55'']], a<ref name="Article" /> [[May 9]], [[2002]]<ref name="HNN55 date">{{JCFCite|url=posts/6774521|board=Literature|thread=The Official HoloNetNews Discussion Thread|user=IAmTheDarkSide|date=2002-05-09|time=4:55 PM|archivedate=20200305125429}}</ref> issue of the [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News website]]. The article that references the government, entitled "Cadinth Loyalty Uncertain After Jedi Death,"<ref name="Article" /> was written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and [[Paul Ens]].<ref name="HNN Credits">{{HoloNetNewsWeb||credits.html|Credits}}</ref>
